observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
analyze and conduct a thorough review of the outstanding accounts payable, retrieve pertinent supporting documents, and check/validate the existence of actual claimants by sending confirmations; and
effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify other payables erroneously recorded in the Accounts Payable following the proposed adjusting journal entry, if deemed necessary:
Accounts Payable				785,477.62
Due to Officers and Employees			389,740.93
Due to GSIS					    2,203.12
Due to National Government Agencies		  10,733.64
Due to LGUs 				            382,799.93
To reclassify payables erroneously charged to the  accounts payable account

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
determine the nature/causes of the non-moving and negative balances and prepare the necessary adjustments to come up with reconciled and correct account balances;
provide sufficient supporting documents to adjustments taken up in the books, and henceforth submit the same together as attachments to approved JEVs on a monthly basis; and
adhere to the requirement of fair presentation of accounts by adequately disclosing relevant information in the Notes to FS.

The Municipal Accountant committed to implement the recommendation by validating and reviewing individual non-moving payables, providing sufficient documentary proof to support adjustments to the account, and by making appropriate disclosures of relevant information on the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Due to BIR account;
file an application for a tax refund or request for the over-remitted amount to be credited against future tax liabilities; and
henceforth, ensure that proper reconciliation is made to verify withholding and remittance amounts before filing and payment to the BIR.

The Municipal Accountant explained that deductions for taxes withheld on compensation were made from the monthly salary payrolls for easier consideration of the amounts withheld. On the other hand, for the other deficiencies noted, the LGU will render the necessary adjusting entries and strictly comply with the BIR provision on the withholding of taxes at the time of payable recognition.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed take up the non-current portion of Loans Payable – Domestic under non-current liabilities to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipal Government in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

The Municipal Accountant explained that the Municipal Government adopted the current format of the Statement of Financial Position as generated by the eNGAs, hence the non-presentation of the non-current portion of the loans payable, and by the time being, their office will manually prepare the financial statements which are compliant with the requirements of IPSAS 1.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recognition of cost of improvements and major repairs to the specific PPE account instead of Repairs and Maintenance Expense pursuant to IPAS 17; and provide and compute the related depreciation expense of the building; and
Strictly adhere to IPAS 17 on capitalization of assets for proper reporting of the balances in the Financial Statement

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to refrain from recommending for approval, and the Municipal Mayor deny the approval of loan applications of LGU employees to lending institutions if this would cause the employee’s net take-home pay to fall below or is already below the minimum set by regulations.

The Municipal Accountant stated that the Municipal Government will strictly observe the provisions of the General Appropriations Act by conducting a review on the provisions of the individual Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the lending entities to include the submission of a Certification on the Net Take Home Pay as part of the loan application process.

We recommended, and the GAD Focal Point System agreed to:
initiate the conduct of gender analysis and thereafter prepare the GAD Agenda of the Municipal Government based on the identified gender gaps or issues therein;
ensure compliance with the established submission timelines of the GPB and GAD AR as outlined in COA Circular No. 2014-001;
establish corrective measures to ensure that its GAD programs, projects, and activities are efficiently and judiciously consistent with the provisions of R.A. No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars Nos. 2013-a and 2014-1; and
assign the appropriate Responsibility Center (RC) for GAD to ensure proper tracking, monitoring, and reporting of gender-related expenditures, in accordance with the procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2021-008.

The GAD TWG Leader explained that some of the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget that were partially and not implemented were due to the following issues that occurred: i. the procurement of identified supplies, medicines and other commodities with the Rural Health Unit being the responsible center, did not find a supplier to commit to the procurement, and ii. other trainings and activities lack time of implementation due to converging activities and formulation of training design was not realized.
Moreover, the GAD TWG Leader committed that a three-year term GAD Agenda for the Municipal Government (which will be linked to the GAD Database) will be formulated through capacitating the GFPS in gender analysis by partaking involvement in trainings and workshop.
The GAD TWG Leader added that since corrective measures as stated above will be ensured, such issues will be carefully monitored and evaluated so in future will be avoided.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the pertinent provisions of the IPSAS 1 and 2 regarding the disclosure of relevant information in the Notes to FS for these to be fairly presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the Trust Fund books to determine the transactions that brought about the Government Equity balance and effect the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, observe the proper recording of transactions in the Trust Fund books, and the preparation of its financial statements.
We recommended that the Accountant strictly follow the disclosure requirements in Item 47 of IPSAS 24 and Annex G, Note 33 of COA Circular 2016-004.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Budget Officer in the preparation of annual and supplemental budgets:

limit the allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) to the Office of the Municipal Mayor; and

comply with the 2% statutory limitation for EME based on the actual collections of real property taxes in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160.

Municipal Accountant strengthen control on the disbursement of funds by disapproving payments charged against the EME in excess of the 2% limitation.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer properly monitor and evaluate the project implementation and undertake on-site validations in order for the Municipality to report data reflective of the accurate accomplishments of the projects being implemented.

We recommended that the management utilize the LDRRMF strictly for the implementation of projects and activities exclusively for disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery as envisioned in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: a. the Municipal Accountant to regularly establish RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and the same must be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Treasurer at year-end, in adherence to Section 20, Volume I of NGAs for LGUs; and b. the Municipal Treasurer to regularly update the list of delinquent real property taxes as a basis for recording receivables in the books and enforce the collection of unpaid delinquent taxes through tax campaigns.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer to reconcile the balances per book with that of the inventory report; b. require the Accounting Office and the General Service Office to set up and maintain subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively for all PPE items; and c. direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation expense periodically for all depreciable assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate special account for each economic enterprise of the Municipality and prepare a complete set of Financial Statements in order to provide adequate information on the financial self-sustainability of the LEEs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) direct the official concerned to explain the lapses in their responsibilities which caused the delays in the implementation of the projects; b) direct the officials concerned to comply with the required detailed engineering activities for every infrastructure project included in the Annual Procurement Plan to avoid the incurrence of delay in the completion of the project; c) submit documents for the termination of the contract for the construction of the slaughterhouse; d) consider blacklisting contractors from participating in the future bidding process for one (1) year; and e) require the Municipal Engineer to submit data to the Municipal Accountant, for the computation and enforcement of liquidated damages for projects with delayed completion.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensures the implementation of PPAs not yet implemented in CY 2022, and subsequently spearheads the appropriate planning of priority development projects to come up with PPAs that are well-planned and procurement/implementation-ready as mandated by laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRMC to identify programs/projects/activities that will strengthen the capacity of the LGU for disaster risk reduction and management and ensure the full implementation of the PPAs within the timeframe to optimize the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resource Management Officer and other concerned officials to: a) prepare the Job Order Memorandum and Job Order Contract of Service Memorandum and in consistent with the requirements under Rule XI of CSC Memorandum No. 40, series of 1998 as amended by SCS Memorandum No. 15, series of 1999; b) consider the physical and mental fitness of personnel hired as JO and CoS taking into account the nature of work or services to be performed to ensure that government resources are expenses prudently; and c) assess its existing organizational structure so that reasonable plantilla personnel will be established in order to fulfill its mandate of providing basic services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to reconcile the balances per books with that of the inventory report; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant, Inventory Committee, and other concerned personnel to initiate necessary steps in the turnover of completed barangay infrastructure projects located within the territorial jurisdiction of barangays in order to transfer the responsibility in managing and maintaining the properties; (c) require the Accounting Office and the GSO to set up and maintain subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively, for all PPE items; (d) require disclosure of Local Road Networks to support general ledger account balances and improve internal control over property management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the each head of the department or office to submit their budget proposal and the municipal treasurer her statement of income and expenditures on or before the fifteenth (15th) of July of each year, and submit the executive budget to the Sanggunian not later than the sixteenth (16th) of October, in order to avoid delay in the enactment of budget and to prevent delay in the implementation of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Annual Budget for CY 2020 was prepared delayed from the prescribed period resulting in delayed implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Municipal officials were still in the process of conducting the Local Road Inventory and gathering data on road network; the required documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the municipal officials concerned and the Neighbourhood Shelter Homeowner’s Association (NASA) to properly monitor, supervise and coordinate, speed up the construction process of the remaining Core Shelters so that it will be used by the families/beneficiaries in order to have a decent shelters and to protect them from the harmful effect of disasters.
observations.reason: The municipality was still in the process of construction of remaining 40 units; no status report submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary correction and adjustment of the affected
assets and income accounts. Also, require the Municipal General Services
Officer to prepare and submit Report on the Physical Count of Inventories
and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE such as Parks, Plazas and
Monuments, etc. and to issue property acknowledgement receipt and
inventory custodian slip for the properties received by the end-user.

Moreover, oblige the Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to
prepare the records for inventories and PPE, to facilitate the reconciliation of
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
officials to strictly follow the rules and regulations in the appropriation,
utilization, recording and reporting of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund (LDRRMF) in order to ascertain the propriety and
correctness of the fund.

Moreover, oblige the Treasurer and Accountant to submit paid vouchers and
its supporting documents pertaining to LDRRMF to assure the legality and
validity of the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
officials to strictly follow the rules and regulations in the appropriation,
utilization, recording and reporting of the 20% DF in order to ascertain the
propriety and correctness of the fund. Also, require the accountant to record
the fund as Special Account in the General Fund with specific subsidiary
ledger to ease the verification and reconciliation of the account.

Moreover, compel the municipal officials concerned to submit the necessary
reports to assure the correctness of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Accountant to submit their joint certification under oath to
support the sources and availability of funds for the supplemental and
realignment of budget. Also, submit valid reason for the changes of budget
and or appropriation in order to ascertain the validity and legality of the
ordinances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the municipal
officials concerned to strictly follow the provisions of IRR of R.A. No. 9184
regarding procurement process in order to achieve transparency,
accountability, efficiency and economy.

Moreover, compel the end-user unit to prepare their individual PPMP, and
the Budget Officer and BAC to strictly follow Appendix 2 of the
implementing rules and regulations of Government Procurement Reform
Act.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Award Committee to submit all contracts within five (5) working days from
perfection for early review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the BAC, Treasurer,
Accountant and end-user to submit the necessary documents and reports
that will support the procurement of rice to ascertain the propriety and
validity of the disbursement and its distribution.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to verify the balance of P4,060,031.40 deposited at PNB-Guiuan
Branch under Account No. 313470002211 and submit a report of liquidation
or certification of full liquidation of the fund transfer and/or return the
remaining balance, if any to DSWD. Also, instruct the treasurer to close the
PNB account and transfer the amount of P4,060,031.40 to LBP Branch which
is an authorized government depository bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to
strictly follow the rules and regulation in granting monetization of
accumulated leave credit in order to assure the regularity and validity of the
transaction.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive and require the Municipal Treasurer to record all bank account transactions cast in the cashbooks, and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit all bank reconciliation statements and effect adjustments of the incomplete reconciling items in the ledger in order to present fairly the cash in bank accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer to record all bank account transactions cast in the cashbooks, and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit all bank reconciliation statements and effect adjustments of the incomplete reconciling items in the ledger in order to present fairly the cash in bank accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant require the municipal officials and employees to settle their cash advances, issue a demand letter to enforce liquidation of their corresponding cash advances to avoid misuse or personal use of government funds.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the municipal accountant to demand for the liquidation of fund transfer and record the total amount of P266,530.00 in the general and subsidiary ledger of the account Due from LGU in order to present fairly the account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: There was issuance of demand for the liquidation. The demand for the letter was made by the accountant, there was no enforcement of the liquidation, there was an additional fund transfer of P10,000.00 to barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO to comply with the requirements in development of MDRRMP using the NDRRMP template as guide in order that the LDRRMFIP has a basis on its program, projects and activities and to achieve a good governance of the disaster fund.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Official to strictly follow the above rules and regulation in the utilization of LDRRMF and Special Trust Fund to avoid incurrence of excessive and unapproved usage of fund and to ensure efficient utilization of LDRRM Fund.
observations.reason: Prior years' unexpended LDRRMF were not included in the LDRRMFIP and the utilization report; the issue was addressed by implementing the rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly follow COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the maintenance of separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for QRF, MOOE and CO for proper accounting and monitoring of LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Compr ehensive RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO were submitted to COA for verification by the budget officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the municipal officials concerned to exert effort to implement and execute the development projects, in order to attain the desired outcomes on the socio-economic and environmental management projects that will benefit the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Efforts were exerted to implement and execute development projects; implementation improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as head of the municipal development council, create or institutionalize a monitoring committee that will coordinate, monitor, and evaluate the implementation of development programs and projects in order to avoid delayed execution and non-implementation of development programs and projects.
observations.reason: A monitoring committee was institutionalized to coordinate, monitor and evaluate implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive require the Terminal Management Office to exert efforts in the enforcement of collection in order to generate additional revenues that can be used for development projects and programs.
observations.reason: Enforcement efforts were exerted and additional revenues were generated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the municipal officials concerned to monitor with regards to the selection of contractors to avoid awarding of multiple contracts to a single contractor, and to properly supervise and monitor the implementation of the awarded contract to prevent the occurrence of delay in the construction of projects. Also, we recommended the application of liquidated damages for the delayed execution of works on the awarded projects and termination or rescission of contracts for those who incurred 10% negative slippage as provided for in the contract agreement to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Implemented; monitoring and supervision practices improved and liquidated damages applied where appropriate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive require the immediate head of the offices to desist their job order workers from performing the task of a regular employee and limit their number per office. Also, require the two revenue collection clerk to return to their position as collector in order to augment the collection of revenue for the municipality and require the job order to refrain from collecting market fees.
observations.reason: Implemented; job order assignments were revised and job orders ceased from performing regular employee tasks and collection duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer, Market Supervisor and concerned municipal officials to prepare the Contract of Lease for market stalls to determine the legitimacy of the persons occupying the market premises/stalls.
observations.reason: Implemented; contracts of lease for the market stalls were prepared and monitoring instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor require the Market Supervisor and Municipal Treasurer to submit reports on market stall rentals to the Municipal Accountant, so that proper recording of the corresponding accounts in the financial statements shall be made to avoid understatement of the same.
observations.reason: Implemented; Market Supervisor and Municipal Treasurer submitted reports and receivables were recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account in the infrastructure assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipality will hire additional manpower and procure additional equipment for data gathering and survey activities and intensify data gathering on activities and road network details.
observations.reason: Municipal Officials were still on the process of conducting inventory and gathering data on road network details.

observations.recommendation:

Also, require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the subsidiary ledgers and cashbooks in order to attain the accuracy of its balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the municipal
officials and employees to settle their cash advances, issue a demand letter to enforce
for their liquidation of their corresponding cash advances to avoid misuse or
personal use of government funds.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the municipal accountant
to demand for the liquidation of fund transfer to barangays and record the total
amount of P266,530.00 in the general and subsidiary ledger of the account Due from
LGU in order have a proper accounting, reporting and liquidation of fund
transferred and to present fairly the account in the financial statements.

be coursed thru the corresponding inventory account and issuance thereof shall be

General Services Officer to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and the
Requisition and Issue Slip to the Municipal Accountant for recording the
transactions in the Journal Entry Voucher and books of account to reflect the proper
issuance of inventories in order to ascertain the proper recording of the inventory and
expense account and to attain the fair presentation of the account in the financial
statements.

We also recommended the regular or monthly submission of the Journal
Entry Vouchers for all transactions in order to conduct timely verification of the
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the MDRRMO to
comply with the requirements in development of MDRRMP using the NDRRMP
template as guide in order that the LDRRMFIP has a basis on its program, projects
and activities and to achieve a good governance of the disaster fund.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Official to
strictly follow the above rules and regulation in the utilization of LDRRMF and

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to strictly follow COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the maintenance of
separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for QRF,
MOOE and CO for proper accounting and monitoring of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as head of the disaster
council require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to
prepare monthly the sources and utilization of LDRRMF duly certified by the Municipal
Accountant in order to verify and monitor on time the status of fund.
Also, require the conduct and maintain complete and updated inventory of Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) and Climate Change Adaptation (CCA)
resources and services to assure the availability of complete data and information of
Municipality’s Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.

attaining the Philippine Development Plan’s goal of inclusive growth and
poverty reduction, and promoting good governance at the local level.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal officials
concerned to allocate one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for
the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of
LCPC as provided for under RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2012-120 in order not to deprive the children in the administration of juvenile
justice and welfare.

We also recommend the submission of the above documents for validation as
to propriety of the activities of the program.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct municipal officials
concerned to properly allocate the five percent (5%) Gender and Development as
provided for under the provisions of RA 9710 and the regulations stipulated in
DILG Joint Memorandum Circulars and in accordance with the Harmonized
Gender and Development in order not to hamper the enhancement on the
effectiveness of the existing guidelines.

We also recommend the separation of budget of the Local Council for the
Protection of Children (LCPC) from the GAD budget as provided for under the
existing laws and regulations.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to strictly comply with the needed supporting documents on the procurement of
fuel, oil and lubricants and usage of government vehicle, and submit the monthly
reports in order to ascertain the authority on the use of government vehicles and
to properly determine, monitor and control the monthly fuel consumption.
Moreover, purchases of fuel, oil and lubricants for stock should be recorded in
the Fuel, Oil and Lubricant account, issuance is therefore credited to expense
account through the submission of monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) by the general services office, proper maintenance of ledger and
stock cards should be made by the accounting unit and the general services
office.

We also recommend to stop payment for the reimbursement of fuel incurred
by the employees during their official travel, usage of fuel for government motor
vehicle should be properly requisitioned and approved first by the concerned
municipal officials before they will go on travel, in order to ascertain the validity
and propriety of the transactions.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of disallowance in the
conduct of post audit of the said transactions.
